model_specific_findings.evidence: assert 0.0 <= p["position"][0] <= terrain_width and assert 0.0 <= p["position"][1] <= terrain_depth

findings.evidence: assert 0.0 <= p["position"][0] <= terrain_width and assert 0.0 <= p["position"][1] <= terrain_depth
findings.recommendation: Given the feature/terrain-world-foundation branch, the scatter engine likely now returns world-space coordinates. Update assertions to account for terrain origin offsets (e.g., terrain_origin_x, terrain_origin_y) or verify if local-space is still intended for this pipeline.
findings.title: Stale position bounds assertion assumes local-space coordinates

model_specific_findings.evidence: radius = placements[0]["radius"] assumes the planner returns a radius key in the placement dict.

findings.evidence: radius = placements[0]["radius"] assumes the planner returns a radius key in the placement dict.
findings.recommendation: Verify that _plan_map_location_anchors actually includes radius in its return payload. If not, compute expected radius from terrain size or adjust the test to match the actual return schema.
findings.title: Unverified dictionary key access in anchor planner test

model_specific_findings.evidence: The test patches internal helpers but does not mock bpy.context, bpy.ops, or other runtime Blender dependencies that handle_generate_multi_biome_world likely invokes.

findings.evidence: The test patches internal helpers but does not mock bpy.context, bpy.ops, or other runtime Blender dependencies that handle_generate_multi_biome_world likely invokes.
findings.recommendation: Add patch("bpy.context") or mock the specific Blender API calls used by the handler to prevent AttributeError or RuntimeError in headless CI environments.
findings.title: Missing Blender context/API mocks in multi-biome world test

model_specific_findings.evidence: assert coarse[2, 2][0] > fine[2, 2][0] and assert coarse[2, 2][1] < fine[2, 2][1] assumes the biome weight array has at least 2 channels.

findings.evidence: assert coarse[2, 2][0] > fine[2, 2][0] and assert coarse[2, 2][1] < fine[2, 2][1] assumes the biome weight array has at least 2 channels.
findings.recommendation: Add a guard or assertion for coarse.shape[-1] >= 2 before indexing, or use named channel indices/constants to prevent IndexError if biome configuration changes.
findings.title: Hardcoded channel index assumption in splatmap weight test
